title: Unable to use Skopeo in a non-privileged container after #624
body:
```
$ docker run --rm -it --entrypoint sh skopeo-build
/ # /skopeo copy docker://a docker://b
Error during unshare(CLONE_NEWUSER): Operation not permitted
ERRO[0000] error parsing PID "": strconv.Atoi: parsing "": invalid syntax 
ERRO[0000] (unable to determine exit status)
$ docker run --privileged --rm -it --entrypoint sh skopeo-build
/ # /skopeo copy docker://a docker://b
FATA[0001] Error determining manifest MIME type for docker://a:latest: Error reading manifest latest in docker.io/library/a: errors:
denied: requested access to the resource is denied
unauthorized: authentication required
```

comments.body: `unshare` might be blocked by seccomp.  Could you try with --security-opt seccomp=unconfined?

Setting seccomp=undefined indeed seems to work as well.
Though it's not something that helps overly much in the case of our CI/CD pipelines, as there's - by design - no way to request modified permissions there.
@ananace could you check if this PR fixes the issue for you https://github.com/containers/skopeo/pull/638 ?
@giuseppe Seems to solve the issue, yes.
